ab: Inacila^, tayra kako i niyaro’ a miliso’ ci akongan.
ch: 昨天，我去部落拜訪爺爺。
en: Yesterday, I went to visit my grandpa in the tribe.

ab: Halafin to kami a caay kasaso’araw, maoron kako i ciiraan.
ch: 我們很久沒有見面了，我很想念他。
en: We haven’t seen each other for a long time and I miss him a lot.

ab: Ma’araw ni akong kako ri, pakaen ciira to sinar i takowanan, ato pa-ucya^ i takowanan.
ch: 爺爺一看到我，就請我吃西瓜，請我喝茶。
en: As soon as grandpa saw me, he invited me to eat watermelon and drink tea.

ab: Macedem ko nipalomaan ni akong a sinar, kaeso’.
ch: 爺爺種的西瓜很甜，真的很好吃。
en: The watermelon grown by grandpa is very sweet and really delicious.

ab: Maola:h kako to u-cya^ ni akong, fangsis a nanomen.
ch: 我也喜歡爺爺的茶，也很好喝。
en: I also like grandpa’s tea, it’s tasted good too.

ab: O kalahokan sato, makomod kako aci ama: a misakalahok.
ch: 中午的時候，我和奶奶一起煮午餐。
en: At noon, I cooked lunch with my grandma.

ab: Adi:hay ko nisacacakan ni ama: to sinafel, ira ko foting, o ’ayam, o tamorak, ato ira haca^ ko kacomoli^.
ch: 奶奶煮了很豐盛的午餐，有魚、有雞、有南瓜、還有蝸牛。
en: Grandma cooked a big lunch, including fish, chicken, pumpkin, and snails.

ab: Herek no lahok i, talapadatengan kami a midateng.
ch: 吃完午餐後，我們去菜園摘菜。
en: After lunch, we went to the vegetable garden to pick vegetables.

ab: Adihay ko nialaan ni ama: to fonga^, tali^, lokiyo^, pataloma’en sa i takowanan.
ch: 奶奶拿了很多地瓜、芋頭和蕗蕎給我，要我帶回家。
en: Grandma brought me a lot of sweet potatoes, taro and buckwheat, and asked me to take them home.

ab: “Taloma’ to kako, tayni hato ako anoro:ma^ a romi’ad.” han ako caira a palicay.
ch: 我跟他們道別，說下次再去看他們。
en: I said goodbye to them and said I’d see them next time.
